message:
docs: fix robot API JSON schema command descriptions to match nested CLI surface

Updates three robot JSON schema description fields to reflect the actual
implemented CLI command structure:

- wa-robot-accounts.json: 'wa robot accounts' → 'wa robot accounts list'
- wa-robot-workflow-run.json: 'wa robot workflow run <name> --pane <id>'
  → 'wa robot workflow run <name> <pane_id>'
- wa-robot-workflow-status.json: adds [<execution_id>] [--pane <id>]
  [--active] optional args to description

Also updates .beads/issues.jsonl with latest bead tracking notes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -2786,7 +2786,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-3681t.3.7.1","title":"[swarm][handoff] Explicit auto-resolve handoff contract in mission deconfliction messages","description":"Add explicit safe-handoff contract metadata and human-readable guidance to mission deconfliction messages when conflicts are auto-resolved, so losing agents have deterministic next actions. Scope: crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs + targeted tests for message content and thread routing. Validation: focused tests + rch-offloaded cargo check/test evidence.","notes":"Implemented scoped handoff-contract enhancement in `crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs` for child bead `ft-3681t.3.7.1`.

Code changes:
- `generate_conflict_messages` now emits recipient-specific handoff guidance instead of one shared body.
- Added explicit handoff contract block in each message body with deterministic fields:
  - role (`winner` / `loser` / `participant`)
  - action (`retain_assignment` / `yield_assignment` / `hold_and_retry` / `await_manual_resolution` / `observe_resolution`)
  - `thread_id` continuity target
  - continuity guidance sentence
- Added helper `handoff_contract_for_recipient(...)`.
- Extended tests:
  - `conflict_detection_auto_resolved_messages_include_handoff_roles`
  - `conflict_detection_manual_resolution_message_has_explicit_wait_action`
  - strengthened `conflict_detection_deconfliction_message_content` with handoff-contract assertion.

Validation evidence:
1) `rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs` ✅
2) `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-mistydog-3681t-3-7-1 rch exec -- cargo test -p frankenterm-core conflict_detection_auto_resolved_messages_include_handoff_roles -- --nocapture`
   - rch attempted remote offload but failed open to local due rsync churn.
   - build blocked by pre-existing unrelated compile errors:
     - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/command_handoff.rs:131:23` unresolved crate/module `shlex` (E0433)
     - `crates/frankenterm-core/src/tui/command_handoff.rs:140:8` type inference error (E0282)
3) `CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-mistydog-3681t-3-7-1 rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm-core --lib`
   - remote sync fallback to local; run then failed in external toolchain path while compiling vendored openssl (`openssl-sys` build script header expansion / missing `build/expando.c` after local fallback environment churn).

Net: feature slice is implemented and formatted; cargo validation is currently blocked by unrelated workspace/toolchain issues outside touched file.","status":"blocked","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistyDog","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:33:57.641137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:49:40.586699Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coordination","locks","safety","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.7.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.3.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:33:57.641137Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.3.7.2","title":"[ft-3681t.3.7][support] Resource-scope reservation conflicts in mission loop","description":"Bounded implementation slice under ft-3681t.3.7. Add explicit resource-scope conflict detection alongside existing file reservation overlap and bead-claim collision handling in mission_loop, plus tests and event/reason-code coverage. Focused files: crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_events.rs. Use targeted rch-offloaded verification only.","notes":"2026-03-11 IndigoSparrow: Implemented explicit resource-scope reservation conflict detection in mission_loop via detect_conflicts_with_resources(), ConflictType::ResourceReservationOverlap, KnownResourceReservation, resource scope overlap matching, and unit coverage for detect/wildcard/expiry/serde paths. Net code diff is confined to crates/frankenterm-core/src/mission_loop.rs; mission_events reason-code mapping was already present in current HEAD. Validation status: local non-cargo hygiene passed (git diff --check, rustfmt --check on touched files). Required cargo validation is currently infra-blocked because rch workers probe returned RCH-E100 SSH connection failures across all configured workers, so the bead remains in_progress pending offloaded cargo check/test/fmt evidence.","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IndigoSparrow","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:49:21.912849Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-11T04:58:38.499814Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["coordination","locks","safety","swarm"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.3.7.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.3.7","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-11T04:49:21.912849Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4","title":"[epic] Robot API super-surface and automation contracts","description":"# Epic 4: Robot API Super-Surface

Expose machine-first interfaces that cover and exceed NTM's command surface with stronger contracts, richer event semantics, and deterministic automation.","acceptance_criteria":"1. `ft robot` supports NTM-equivalent and superior machine workflows.
2. Event streaming and transactional actions are stable and auditable.
3. API contracts and replay tests prevent protocol drift.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5. [QUALITY-GATE] Persist triage-ready artifacts (stdout/stderr, structured logs, snapshots/fixtures, benchmark outputs where relevant), document exact run commands and artifact paths, and run CPU-intensive cargo validation via rch.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:43.367066Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-02T19:32:02.558493Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["automation","epic","robot-api"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:43.367066Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:48.935107Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:09:49.348534Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1721,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Design note: Robot APIs are a product surface, not tooling afterthought. We are standardizing deterministic schemas, event semantics, and transactional controls so AI-to-AI orchestration is robust and testable.","created_at":"2026-03-02T19:17:55Z"}]}

Validation path uses rch as required, but remote workers are currently unreachable (rch workers probe shows SSH failures) so runs fail open to local; an isolated CARGO_HOME/CARGO_TARGET_DIR fallback compile/test run is active to avoid lock contention.","created_at":"2026-03-03T06:00:31Z"},{"id":1778,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","author":"MistyBarn","text":"Validation results (all via rch, fail-open local due worker connectivity):\n1) cargo test -p frankenterm alias_ -- --nocapture: now fails on pre-existing unrelated test compile error in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs around Commands::Status initializer missing new field  (E0063).\n2) cargo check --workspace --all-targets: fails due pre-existing unrelated errors in frankenterm-core (e.g., mission_agent_mail LaunchOutcome missing fields, fleet_launcher mutable borrow, swarm_work_queue type mismatches).\n3) cargo clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings: fails immediately on pre-existing clippy violations in frankenterm/blob-leases/src/simple_tempdir.rs.\n\nLocal slice compiles through my added alias parser tests until blocked by above external errors; I removed one introduced assertion issue by switching Option compare to command.is_none().","created_at":"2026-03-03T06:23:13Z"},{"id":1779,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","author":"MistyBarn","text":"Correction/addendum: the unrelated test compile blocker is E0063 at crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:43885 where a Commands::Status initializer is missing field resize after recent command-shape changes.","created_at":"2026-03-03T06:23:19Z"},{"id":2127,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","author":"PeachDesert","text":"PeachDesert taking a follow-up slice on ft-3681t.4.1: fixing robot API schema/registry drift for reservation subcommands and adding regression coverage so the documented robot surface matches the actual nested CLI path (). Heavy validation will use rch per repo policy.","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:05:00Z"},{"id":2128,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","author":"PeachDesert","text":"Correction for my prior note: the concrete CLI path being fixed in this slice is ft robot reservations reserve|release (nested under the reservations command family), not a top-level robot reserve/release surface.","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:07:40Z"},{"id":2130,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","author":"PeachDesert","text":"Expanded the reservation-surface cleanup beyond api_schema.rs: updated docs/json-schema descriptions, docs/integration-guide command table, and robot_types.rs doc comments so the implemented nested CLI shape is consistently documented as ft robot reservations list|reserve|release. Added a focused regression test in api_schema.rs to lock the nested command paths.","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:15:30Z"},{"id":2132,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","author":"PeachDesert","text":"Verification note for the reservation command-path cleanup slice: jq validation passed for the touched JSON schema files; rustfmt --check passed for crates/frankenterm-core/src/api_schema.rs and crates/frankenterm-core/src/robot_types.rs; git diff --check passed for the touched files. I attempted targeted cargo validation via rch twice. First run was too broad and compiled all crate test targets; second run was narrowed to cargo test -p frankenterm-core --lib reservation_endpoints_use_nested_robot_command_paths -- --nocapture. In both cases rch selected a worker but remote execution failed with SSH/connectivity errors and failed open locally. I stopped the local fallback compile after an extended wait with no test result so this slice ends with code/docs updated and lightweight validation complete, but without a finished cargo test result.","created_at":"2026-03-11T06:21:20Z"}]}

 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.1.1","title":"[robot][compile] Fix Commands::Status initializer drift in alias/parser tests","description":"Scope: unblock ft-3681t.4.1 test compilation by updating stale Commands::Status initializers after recent command-shape changes (resize field).\n\nDeliverables:\n1) Patch stale test initializer(s) in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs to include required fields.\n2) Add/adjust targeted parser test assertions if needed for shape stability.\n3) Validate with rch-offloaded targeted cargo test/check for frankenterm crate.\n\nNon-goals: no broader robot behavior changes; compile-unblock only.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"MistySparrow","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:57:16.815244Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-03T21:10:22.104101Z","closed_at":"2026-03-03T21:10:22.104017Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1.1","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-03T20:57:16.815244Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1790,"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1.1","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"MistySparrow completion update (2026-03-03): applied minimal compile-unblock patch in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs test helper: added missing resize: false to stale Commands::Status initializer in auto_font_install_respects_version_dry_run_and_setup_font.

Validation (offloaded via rch as required):
- rch exec -- cargo check -p frankenterm --tests
  - remote worker selection/sync succeeded, then worker connection failed and rch fail-opened to local execution.
  - compile now proceeds past the previous Commands::Status missing-field error and fails later on pre-existing unrelated frankenterm-core errors in swarm_scheduler.rs (queue.config() method missing and pattern-binding errors) plus one existing warning in metrics.rs.

Net: this slice removes the known status-initializer drift without broad behavior changes; remaining failures are outside this slice.","created_at":"2026-03-03T21:10:07Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.1.2","title":"[robot][compile] Resolve remaining Commands::Status initializer drift and stabilize robot alias tests","description":"Follow-on slice for ft-3681t.4.1: fix any remaining Commands::Status initializer shape drift in parser/alias tests after robot alias expansion, keep NTM-compat robot command surface tests green, and document validation evidence. Includes targeted test updates in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs and rch-offloaded validation runs.","notes":"2026-03-04 CoralCastle: implemented robot alias-surface stabilization in crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs by expanding quick-start alias guidance and adding parser tests for additional aliases (panes, tail, write, watch, grep, eventlog). Validation: rustfmt --edition 2024 --check crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs PASS. rch exec -- env CARGO_HOME=/tmp/ft-coralcastle-cargo-home CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/ft-coralcastle-target-robot cargo test -p frankenterm alias_ -- --nocapture PASS (11 alias tests in src/main.rs). Workspace-wide rch check/clippy/fmt attempts were initiated; remote worker connectivity intermittently failed (fail-open local) and workspace-wide runs remain noisy/expensive under concurrent swarm compile load with known unrelated formatting/compile debt in other files.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"CoralCastle","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:11:09.180054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-04T01:32:17.641546Z","closed_at":"2026-03-04T01:32:17.641520Z","close_reason":"Implemented alias-surface stabilization in main.rs and validated targeted alias tests","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","ntm-convergence","robot-api"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-3681t.4.1.2","depends_on_id":"ft-3681t.4.1","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-04T01:11:09.180054Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-3681t.4.2","title":"[robot] Streaming event/wait interfaces for deterministic automation","description":"Implement streaming event subscriptions and wait primitives with filtering, correlation IDs, cursor checkpoints, and bounded replay so autonomous agents coordinate against real system state with deterministic ordering/reconnect semantics rather than polling heuristics.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Event streams support filtering, cursoring, checkpoint resume, and bounded replay.
2. Wait primitives are race-safe, timeout-safe, and cancellation-safe.
3. Ordering, dedupe, and reconnect/restart semantics are deterministic for automation clients.
4. Backpressure/flow-control behavior prevents silent event loss or unbounded memory growth under high throughput.
Q1. [QUALITY-GATE] Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge cases, boundary conditions, and explicit failure/error paths for all touched logic.
Q2. [QUALITY-GATE] Add integration tests that validate cross-component contracts (mux/orchestrator/robot/policy/connectors as applicable), including at least one degraded-path scenario.
Q3. [QUALITY-GATE] Add deterministic end-to-end scenario scripts that exercise real workflows, including at least one failure-injection case and one recovery case.
Q4. [QUALITY-GATE] Emit detailed structured logs during tests and e2e runs with fields including timestamp, subsystem/component, correlation_id, scenario_id, inputs, decisions/actions, outcomes, and reason_code/error_code.
Q5.  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.4","title":"[asupersync][verify] End-to-end runtime migration scripts and artifact contracts","description":"Author comprehensive e2e scripts dedicated to asupersync migration quality gates with detailed logging and artifact schemas.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. E2E script suite covers happy-path, degraded-path, and rollback-path runtime behaviors.\n2. Scripts integrate with e2e artifact library and produce deterministic manifests.\n3. Unit tests validate script helpers and artifact schema checks.\n4. Integration tests ensure scripts are CI-compatible and idempotent.\n5. E2E outputs include structured logs, timing metrics, and failure signatures by default.","notes":"E2E scripts should be operator-grade diagnostics, not smoke tests. Heavy compile/test/benchmark/soak workloads for this bead must run via rch exec -- <command>, with reproducible command records and artifact paths captured in completion evidence.  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","title":"[asupersync][verify] Unified unit/integration/e2e harness and structured logging contract","description":"Define a single cross-suite harness and structured logging contract so unit, integration, and e2e runtime tests emit comparable, replayable evidence across crates.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define and version a common test-event schema for runtime validation logs (timestamp, run_id, scenario_id, seed, scope_id, operation, event, outcome, error_class, correlation_id, details).\n2. Provide shared helpers/macros so unit, integration, and e2e suites emit schema-compliant events by default.\n3. Unit tests validate schema encoders/decoders, required-field enforcement, and backward-compatible parsing rules for artifacts.\n4. Integration tests verify cross-crate artifact aggregation and deterministic replay index generation.\n5. E2E scripts enforce logging completeness checks and fail when required causal events are missing.\n6. Schema and helper contracts must support secret-safe redaction metadata fields and no-leak validation hooks without breaking traceability.\n7. Heavy test runs and artifact generation are executed via rch exec -- <command> with reproducible command evidence.","notes":"Without a unified harness, logs become incomparable and failures become expensive to triage. This bead standardizes evidence quality before deeper verification work, and it enforces comparable diagnostics for user-critical CLI, robot, watch, and session workflows. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T22:47:06.026926Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-26T07:23:38.537754Z","closed_at":"2026-02-26T07:23:38.537705Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["asupersync","e2e-tests","harness","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.1.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.2.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.6","title":"[asupersync][verify] Crash/restart persistence invariants and recovery replay gate","description":"Validate crash-consistency and restart recovery semantics across runtime-migrated paths so users do not lose critical session/capture/search state under abrupt termination, restart loops, or partial I/O failure.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define persistence invariants for capture pipelines, session state, search indexes, event queues, and control-plane acknowledgements across crash/restart boundaries.\n2. Add unit tests for checkpoint/flush/commit helpers and state-reconciliation logic, including deterministic seed/replay fixtures.\n3. Add integration tests that inject abrupt termination (kill/restart), partial writes, and startup recovery flows, asserting no silent state loss or corruption.\n4. Include explicit failure-injection matrix scenarios (I/O faults, timeout/cancellation interruptions, degraded startup paths, and corrupted checkpoint inputs) with expected outcomes.\n5. Add end-to-end recovery scripts covering user-critical workflows (CLI, robot, watch, session restore, search, remote paths) before and after crash/restart events.\n6. Require rich structured diagnostics logs and artifact manifests (timeline, recovery decisions, data-repair actions, residual anomalies) for every scenario.\n7. Heavy recovery, stress, and soak-like validation runs must execute via rch exec -- <command> with reproducible evidence records.\n8. Closure requires explicit proof that crash/restart behavior meets user-facing reliability and recovery SLO thresholds.","notes":"This gate closes a common production failure blind spot: compile-green runtime migrations can still lose state during abrupt process death or restart churn. This bead ensures users get deterministic recovery, clear diagnostics, and auditable evidence before cutover acceptance. All diagnostics/logging/artifact outputs in this bead must comply with secret-safe redaction and privacy policy gates defined in ft-e34d9.10.7.5, with evidence captured in validation artifacts.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:22:46.617406Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-22T23:43:33.970853Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["artifacts","asupersync","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","quality-gate","reliability","sequencing","test-coverage","test-logging","user-impact","verification"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.3.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.5.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.6","depends_on_id":"ft-e34d9.10.6.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:28Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
-{"id":"ft-e34d9.10.7","title":"[asupersync][track] Observability, operability, and SLO gates","description":"Operationalize the new runtime with observability and reliability guardrails.

- runtime-aware telemetry schema
- doctor/health surfaces and incident bundle enrichment
- SLOs and automated gates tied to runtime behavior

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.

4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Define runtime telemetry schemas and ensure they are consistent across migrated modules.\n2. Expand health/doctor surfaces with runtime-specific checks and actionable remediation guidance.\n3. Enforce secret-safe observability: structured diagnostics, incident bundles, and evidence artifacts must apply deterministic redaction/privacy policy with audit metadata.\n5. Add comprehensive unit, integration, and e2e validation for observability behavior, remediation guidance quality, and redaction safety.\n6. Heavy verification runs execute via rch exec -- <command> with evidence recorded.","notes":"Migration is incomplete if operators cannot observe and reason about runtime behavior. Include explicit rollback/fallback/recovery validation criteria with pass/fail outcomes for user-facing workflows. Require deterministic fixtures/seeds and replayable test evidence so failures are reproducible across environments. This bead must include comprehensive unit tests for scope-local logic, edge cases, and failure branches with deterministic fixtures and replayable seeds.  {"id":"ft-e34d9.10.7.3","title":"[asupersync][ops] Runtime SLOs, alerts, and automated gate policies","description":"Define runtime-specific SLOs and implement automated alert/gate policies for regression prevention.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
